title: Order-Flow Imbalance Without Price Progress
focus_questions: Is the aggressive flow actually moving price, or only making noise?
Is travel efficiency improving or decaying over repeated attempts?

summary.thesis: One-sided flow is not enough by itself; if imbalance cannot create travel, the market is often absorbing, stalling, or losing edge.
summary.operator_translation: Do not trade delta color alone. Judge flow by what it accomplishes in price.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when the tape looks one-sided but price cannot clear the nearby level or keep making progress.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: repeated same-side aggression or imbalance is visible. Required evidence: weak price progress per burst, repeated stalls at the same boundary, or decaying travel efficiency over time. Do not trade: from flow intensity alone when price cannot travel. Real trigger: either renewed progress that revives continuation, or failure that confirms absorption and stalled edge. Failure point: the market starts converting the same-side aggression into clean travel again.

when_relevant: Price is failing to travel relative to the apparent aggression.
The operator is tempted to equate strong flow with strong edge.
required_evidence: there is visible same-side aggression or imbalance
price progress per burst can be compared across attempts
the nearby boundary or target is not being cleared despite the aggression
confirmation_signals: repeated same-side aggression fails to create meaningful travel
price stalls at the same boundary despite continued imbalance
later attempts become less efficient than earlier attempts
invalidation_signals: price starts clearing the same boundary with clean progress
travel efficiency improves instead of decaying
the supposed absorption branch disappears as same-side progress becomes durable
no_trade_conditions: Do not open from delta color or imbalance count alone.
Do not keep adding when travel efficiency is shrinking.
Do not call continuation healthy if aggression is being absorbed at the same edge.

entry_archetypes.name: absorption failure read
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after repeated same-side aggression keeps failing and the stall turns into a usable failure branch.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if price progress is starting to improve again.

entry_archetypes.name: revived travel confirmation
entry_archetypes.description: Enter only after the market proves that the earlier absorbed aggression has turned back into real travel.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the renewed burst still cannot clear the same boundary.

management_notes: Flow should be judged by what it accomplishes, not only by how loud it looks.
Poor price progress often matters more than another burst of same-side aggression.
Once price progress improves again, hand the read off to a real continuation card.
review_questions: Did the operator trade aggression or actual travel?
Was the market progressing less with each new burst?
Where did absorption start dominating the narrative?
